sections.label: Modern Split Hero
sections.description: High-conversion hero with a strong headline, supporting copy, CTA pair, and media column.

sampleContent.eyebrow: High-converting websites for teams that need to launch faster.
sampleContent.headline: Turn your website into a steady source of qualified leads.
sampleContent.body: Launch polished marketing pages, explain your offer clearly, and guide visitors toward the next step without rebuilding your whole stack.

sections.label: Feature Card Grid
sections.description: Three-value feature section for benefit-led product or service explanations.

sampleContent.heading: Show the practical benefits before asking for the click.
sampleContent.subheading: Feature sections work best when they translate product or service strengths into outcomes visitors can understand quickly.

items.title: Structured sections
items.description: Create a strong page narrative instead of assembling disconnected blocks.

items.title: Responsive by default
items.description: Desktop, tablet, and mobile views stay readable with cleaner spacing and hierarchy.

items.title: Marketing-ready flow
items.description: Support trust, objections, proof, and CTA timing instead of relying on generic layouts.

sections.label: Centered Minimal Hero
sections.description: Copy-led centered hero with no media. Best when there is no strong hero image, or for clean, product-forward and SaaS brands that lead with a sharp headline and a single primary action.

sampleContent.eyebrow: Trusted by teams who ship fast
sampleContent.headline: Everything you need to launch a high-converting page.
sampleContent.body: Clear messaging, a focused call to action, and a layout that guides visitors toward the next step — without a heavy redesign.

sections.label: Story Split About
sections.description: Trust-building about section: a clear story on the left and credibility metric cards on the right. Best when you have proof points or numbers (years, clients, projects) that build confidence.

sampleContent.heading: Build trust with a clear story and a sharper promise.
sampleContent.subheading: About sections should explain who you help, why your approach is credible, and why visitors should keep moving toward the next step.

highlights.body: Helping teams launch, test, and improve digital experiences with less friction.
Structured around messaging clarity, trust signals, and action-oriented page flow.

sections.label: Service Offer Grid
sections.description: Service cards in a three-column grid, each clarifying the offer, fit, and who it's for. Best for presenting 3 distinct services that lead toward an inquiry.

sampleContent.heading: Present your offers in a way that leads naturally to inquiry.
sampleContent.subheading: Service sections should help visitors compare what you do, understand fit, and move toward contacting your team.

items.title: Strategy
items.description: Clarify messaging, conversion paths, and page priorities before design refinements.
items.ideal: Ideal for: repositioning, launch prep, and campaign pages.

items.title: Design System
items.description: Create reusable section patterns so teams can publish faster with less inconsistency.
items.ideal: Ideal for: multi-page sites, recurring campaigns, and content teams.

items.title: Optimization
items.description: Improve existing pages with stronger trust signals, CTA placement, and responsive readability.
items.ideal: Ideal for: performance lifts, audits, and post-launch improvements.

sections.label: Quote Grid
sections.description: Three social-proof quote cards in a row to reduce hesitation before the next CTA. Best when you have several short testimonials with attributions.

sampleContent.heading: Teams use this builder to ship marketing pages with more confidence.
sampleContent.subheading: Testimonials help reduce hesitation and give visitors confidence before they contact your team.

items.quote: “We replaced long design cycles with a repeatable system and started publishing campaign pages in hours, not weeks.”
“The structure made it much easier to keep our messaging sharp and our calls to action consistent across the site.”
“It feels like a practical builder for digital marketing, not just a canvas with random blocks.”

sections.label: Banner CTA
sections.description: A strong end-of-page conversion prompt on a bold gradient banner with two actions. Best for the final push after proof and FAQ.

sampleContent.heading: Ready to publish a page that actually supports your pipeline?
sampleContent.body: Use this section near the end of the page to give visitors one confident next step.

sections.label: Stacked FAQ
sections.description: A single column of question/answer cards that resolves pricing, process, and timing objections before contact. Best for up to ~6 Q&As.

sampleContent.heading: Common questions before people contact you.
sampleContent.subheading: FAQ sections reduce friction by answering pricing, process, and timing questions before they become objections.

items.question: How quickly can we launch a page?
items.answer: Most teams can adapt a starter or section-based page in a single working session, then refine copy and styling inside the same builder.

items.question: Can this work for different page types?
items.answer: Yes. The template system is meant to support homepage, services, contact, and campaign-style pages with reusable section patterns.

items.question: What should we optimize first?
items.answer: Start with a clear hero, one focused call to action, and proof sections that reduce hesitation for first-time visitors.

sections.label: Split Contact Form
sections.description: Trust-building copy and proof bullets on the left, a lead-capture form card on the right. Best for higher-intent contact at the end of a page.

sampleContent.heading: Invite the inquiry while the intent is still high.
sampleContent.subheading: A good contact section removes friction, explains what happens next, and gives people confidence that reaching out is worth it.
sampleContent.bullet1: Response time: usually within one business day
sampleContent.bullet2: Best for sales inquiries, partnerships, and project discussions
sampleContent.bullet3: Use this section near the end of the page after proof and FAQ

sections.label: Full-Bleed Background Hero
sections.description: Full-bleed background image with a dark overlay and centered white copy. Best when you have a strong, wide, emotionally resonant photo and want a bold, immersive first impression. Requires a quality hero image.

sampleContent.headline: Make a bold first impression that converts.
sampleContent.body: A full-bleed hero puts your strongest visual front and center, with a single clear action layered on top.

sections.label: Image Split About
sections.description: Heading and story copy on the left, a single authentic photo on the right. Best when you have a real, relevant image of the team, space, or work. Requires an image.

sampleContent.heading: A team that cares about the outcome, not just the output.
sampleContent.subheading: Put a real, human photo next to a clear story about who you help and why your approach works.

sections.label: Minimal Centered CTA
sections.description: A clean centered prompt with a headline and a single action, on the page background (no image or banner). Best for product-forward and SaaS pages that want a quiet, confident close.

sampleContent.heading: Ready when you are.
sampleContent.body: Start in minutes — no heavy setup, no long contracts.

sections.label: Centered Story About
sections.description: A centered narrative about section with no image or metric cards — just a clear, confident story. Best as a universal fallback when there is no strong photo or proof numbers, or for mission-led brands that lead with their why.

sampleContent.heading: We exist to make something genuinely useful.
sampleContent.subheading: Tell the story of who you help and why your approach is different, in a few confident sentences. Keep it human, specific, and free of filler.

sections.label: Feature Two-Column
sections.description: A two-column feature grid for exactly two benefits, kept balanced rather than leaving a gap in a three-column row. Best when there are 1–2 features.

sampleContent.heading: Two strengths, given room to breathe.
sampleContent.subheading: When you have two clear benefits, a two-column layout keeps them balanced and readable.

items.title: Structured sections
items.description: Create a strong page narrative instead of assembling disconnected blocks.

items.title: Responsive by default
items.description: Desktop, tablet, and mobile views stay readable with cleaner spacing and hierarchy.

sections.label: Service Two-Column
sections.description: A two-column service grid for one or two offers, kept balanced instead of stranding a gap in a three-column row. Best when there are 1–2 services.

sampleContent.heading: Two ways we can help.
sampleContent.subheading: A focused pair of offers reads more clearly than a forced third column.

items.title: Strategy
items.description: Clarify messaging, conversion paths, and page priorities before design refinements.
items.ideal: Ideal for: repositioning, launch prep, and campaign pages.

items.title: Optimization
items.description: Improve existing pages with stronger trust signals, CTA placement, and responsive readability.
items.ideal: Ideal for: performance lifts, audits, and post-launch improvements.

sections.label: Single Featured Quote
sections.description: One large, centered testimonial used as a confident proof point. Best when you have a single strong quote rather than several.

sampleContent.quote: “This is the most practical builder we've used — it feels designed for marketing, not just a blank canvas.”

sections.label: Team Grid
sections.description: A responsive grid of team member cards (photo, name, role, optional bio). Column count adapts to how many members there are. Best for introducing the people behind the work.

sampleContent.heading: The people behind the work.
sampleContent.subheading: Introduce the team with a short role and an optional one-line bio.

items.name: Marcus Ong
items.bio: Owns the design system and templates.

sections.label: Gallery Grid
sections.description: A responsive image tile grid. Column count adapts to the number of images. Best for showing work, spaces, products, or events visually.

sampleContent.heading: A look at the work.
sampleContent.subheading: Let the visuals carry the story.

sections.label: Numbered Process
sections.description: A numbered, step-by-step grid explaining how you work. Column count adapts to the number of steps. Best for clarifying an unfamiliar process and reducing perceived risk.

sampleContent.heading: How we work.
sampleContent.subheading: A clear, low-friction path from first contact to launch.

items.title: Discover
items.description: We learn your goals, audience, and the message that matters most.

items.title: Design
items.description: We assemble the page from proven section patterns and refine the copy.

items.title: Launch
items.description: We publish, then iterate based on how real visitors respond.

sections.label: Menu List
sections.description: A categorized list menu (categories with named items, descriptions, and prices). Best for restaurants, cafés, and service price lists.

sampleContent.heading: What we serve.

items.name: Garden Salad
items.description: Crisp greens, citrus vinaigrette

items.name: Soup of the Day
items.description: Ask your server

items.name: Grilled Salmon
items.description: Seasonal vegetables, herb butter

sections.label: Pricing Tiers
sections.description: A grid of pricing tier cards (name, price, feature list, CTA), with an optional 'Most popular' badge on the recommended tier. Column count adapts to the number of tiers. Best for clear plan comparison.

sampleContent.heading: Simple, transparent plans.
sampleContent.subheading: Start free, upgrade when you're ready.

items.name: Starter
items.description: For trying things out.

items.name: Pro
items.description: For growing teams.

sections.label: Standard Header
sections.description: A clean header: wordmark on the left, inline primary navigation and a primary CTA on the right. Navigation resolves from the entity's primary menu.

sections.label: Standard Footer
sections.description: A dark footer: brand wordmark, tagline and contact on the left, grouped navigation columns on the right, with a legal bar (copyright, legal links, credits) below. Uses flex, never grid.

sampleContent.tagline: Helping teams launch better pages, faster.

sections.label: Photo Background CTA
sections.description: A full-bleed background photo with a dark overlay for contrast, centered white headline and action. Best for an atmospheric, high-impact end-of-page push when a strong, on-brand background image is available.

sampleContent.heading: Ready to get started?
sampleContent.body: Tell us what you need and we'll take it from there.

sections.label: Gradient Hero
sections.description: A centered hero on a brand gradient (no photo needed), with white copy and a clear action. Best when there's no strong hero image but you still want a bold, modern, on-brand first impression.

sampleContent.headline: A bolder way to launch your next page.
sampleContent.body: When you don't have a hero photo, a brand gradient still gives a modern, confident first impression.

sections.label: Editorial Split About
sections.description: Asymmetric editorial about: an oversized display heading under a hairline eyebrow with generous whitespace on the left, a tall portrait photo on the right. Equal columns, asymmetric composition. Best for brand/story sections with a strong voice. Requires an image.

sampleContent.heading: A practice built on craft, not noise.
sampleContent.subheading: Lead with a clear point of view. Pair an oversized statement with a single, honest image.

sections.label: Editorial Hero
sections.description: Asymmetric editorial hero: a hairline eyebrow over an oversized display headline with body + actions on the left, a tall image on the right. Equal columns, asymmetric composition. Strong, type-led first impression. Requires an image.

sampleContent.headline: We make ideas worth remembering.
sampleContent.body: A short, confident statement of what you do and who it's for.

sections.label: Editorial Feature List
sections.description: Editorial features: an intro with an oversized heading on the left, a clean hairline-divided list of capabilities on the right. No cards — type-led and calm. Good for a confident, magazine feel.

sampleContent.heading: A focused set of things, done exceptionally.
sampleContent.subheading: Skip the feature soup. List what matters and say it plainly.

items.description: Positioning and messaging that earns attention.

items.description: Interfaces with a point of view.

items.description: Production-grade engineering.

sections.label: Editorial CTA
sections.description: Editorial CTA: an oversized statement on the left, supporting line and actions on the right. Asymmetric, type-led closing band.

sampleContent.heading: Let's make something worth remembering.
sampleContent.body: Tell us what you're working on.

sections.label: Editorial Service List
sections.description: Editorial services: an intro with an oversized heading on the left, a clean hairline-divided list of offerings on the right. Calm, type-led, no cards.

sampleContent.heading: What we can do together.

items.title: Strategy
items.description: Positioning and roadmap.

items.title: Design
items.description: Brand and product design.

items.title: Build
items.description: Engineering and launch.

sections.label: Editorial Pull Quote
sections.description: Editorial testimonial: a single oversized pull-quote with attribution under a hairline eyebrow. Type-led social proof with maximum impact.

sampleContent.quote: “They reframed our whole product story — and the results followed.”

sections.label: Bento Feature Grid
sections.description: Modular bento grid: an intro, then mixed-size rounded tiles in a dense grid. The contemporary 2025 feature layout. Great for SaaS/tech and playful brands.

sampleContent.heading: Everything you need, modular.
sampleContent.subheading: A flexible set of capabilities arranged in a bento grid.
